description_long: This object is a vintage wooden sewing machine model, featuring a robust rectangular wooden base supporting a mechanical assembly of gears and levers. The central mechanism consists of two large, interlocking wooden gears mounted horizontally, with a vertical spindle and a hand crank on top. Several elongated wooden rods extend from the frame, likely serving as levers or supports for the sewing mechanism. The construction is entirely wooden, with visible dowel joints and a natural, unfinished wood texture. The machine's design is open, exposing all moving parts and mechanical linkages, and it lacks any modern electrical or metallic components. The overall appearance is rustic and functional, reminiscent of early mechanical sewing devices.
description: A vintage wooden sewing machine model with exposed gears, levers, and a hand crank, all mounted on a sturdy wooden base. The entire structure is crafted from natural wood, showcasing a rustic and mechanical design.
description_view_0: This view highlights the large interlocking gears and the hand crank on top, with the wooden rods and frame clearly visible.
description_view_1: From this angle, the side profile of the gear assembly and the extended wooden levers are prominent, showing the mechanical linkage.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the length of the base and the alignment of the rods, with the gear teeth and crank partially visible.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back of the gears and the supporting frame, with the rods extending outward and the base's grain visible.
